findings.title: CORS origin widened from allowlist to wildcard "*"
findings.evidence: raw-git-diff.patch changes corsOptions.origin from ["https://admin.example.com"] to "*", allowing any website to make cross-origin requests to this admin API. Confirmed by both the diff hunk and the Diff Drift High-severity flag on node corsOptions.

notes: This is an admin-api project, making a wildcard CORS policy especially risky for an administrative surface.

The diff only changes origin to "*"; it does not set credentials:true in this hunk, so browsers will not send cookies with a wildcard origin. However, the report's concern about credential leakage and the unrestricted cross-origin access remain valid grounds to block.
Diff Drift analyzed only the single changed file (src/cors.ts); no files were skipped as too large.
Branch name agent/fix-cors suggests an intended fix, but the change broadens rather than restricts access — the opposite of an allowlist hardening.

evaluator.note: Benchmark v4 blind run against the engine-v2 binary; packet-only context.
